attractions.name: Antone's Nightclub (Antone's Nightclub)
attractions.description: Established in 1975 by Clifford Antone, this iconic blues venue is a cornerstone of the Austin music scene, famously known as the "Home of the Blues." The club has hosted legends such as B.B. King, Muddy Waters, and Stevie Ray Vaughan, continuing to showcase premier blues, soul, and R&B talent in its downtown location (Antone's Nightclub).
attractions.address: 305 E 5th St, Austin, TX 78701, USA (Antone's Nightclub)
attractions.hours: Monday: 12:00 PM – 2:00 AM, Tuesday: 12:00 PM – 2:00 AM, Wednesday: 12:00 PM – 2:00 AM, Thursday: 12:00 PM – 2:00 AM, Friday: 12:00 PM – 2:00 AM, Saturday: 12:00 PM – 2:00 AM, Sunday: 12:00 PM – 2:00 AM (Tripadvisor, Places to Travel)
attractions.admission: Typically $21–$65 for most shows, with premium acts like Gary Clark Jr. ranging from $141–$258 (SeatGeek). Free monthly concert series are hosted at the Antone’s Stage at Still Austin Whiskey Co. (Antone's Nightclub)
highlights: Famous 'Antone’s Famous Po’ Boys' and beer-steamed 'Cliffy Dogs' served on-site (Antone's Nightclub)
Home to Big Henry’s Vinyl & Gifts, which doubles as the box office and a curated record shop (Antone's Nightclub)
Legendary history of developing local talent like Stevie Ray Vaughan and Gary Clark Jr. (Antone's Nightclub)
Hosting the annual Austin Blues Festival and special 50th-anniversary events (Antone's Nightclub)
Diverse musical lineup including blues, soul, jazz, and indie rock (Antone's Nightclub)
Consistently praised for its high-quality sound system and community-focused atmosphere (Reddit)
attractions.tips: Purchase tickets in advance online as many shows sell out quickly. Be aware of the age policy, as most shows are 21+ unless otherwise specified. Use the nearby Westin Hotel for valet parking or book via SpotHero to secure a spot in the busy downtown area (Antone's Nightclub).
attractions.nearby: The Westin Austin Downtown, 6th Street Entertainment District, Congress Avenue Bridge (Bats), and Lady Bird Lake (Tripadvisor, Austin Insider Blog)

attractions.name: Austin City Limits Live at The Moody Theater (Official Site)
attractions.description: Austin City Limits Live at The Moody Theater is a world-class 2,750-seat live music venue and state-of-the-art production facility located in downtown Austin. It serves as the permanent home for the taping of the acclaimed PBS television series "Austin City Limits," the longest-running music series in American television history (Official Site).
attractions.address: 310 West Willie Nelson Boulevard, Austin, Texas 78701 (Official Site)
attractions.hours: Tours: Fri-Mon 10:00 AM – 2:00 PM; Show Nights: Will call/Bars 6:00 PM, Doors 7:00 PM, Taping/Show 8:00 PM (Official Tours Page, Official FAQ)
attractions.days_open: Tours: Friday–Monday; Performances: Scheduled event dates year-round (Official Tours Page)
attractions.admission: Tours: Adults $25.00, Children 12 & under Free (Tripadvisor, Official FAQ); Concert Tickets: Prices vary by performer and seat tier (Official Site)
highlights: Home of the historic "Austin City Limits" PBS television series tapings (Official Site)
LEED-certified for sustainable development and environmental performance (Ryman Hospitality)
Intimate 2,750-capacity venue with no seat more than 75 feet from the stage (Yelp)
Houses 3TEN ACL Live, a smaller 350-capacity club venue for emerging artists (Official Site)
Features the iconic Willie Nelson statue and curated music artifacts on every level (2nd Street District)
attractions.tips: Arrive at least 30–60 minutes early for security screening and the bag policy. Join the ACL mailing list for a chance to win free tickets to television tapings. Book backstage tours in advance as they are limited and subject to production schedules (Official FAQ, Official Site).
attractions.nearby: W Austin Hotel, Lady Bird Lake, Congress Avenue Bridge (Bat Watching), Violet Crown Cinema, Austin City Hall, and restaurants in the 2nd Street District (2nd Street District)

attractions.name: East Sixth Street (Sixth Street Historic District)
attractions.category: Entertainment District, Historic District, Landmark Wikipedia), Visit Austin
attractions.description: East Sixth Street, famously known as "Dirty Sixth," is the historic heart of Austin's legendary live music and nightlife scene. Lined with late 19th-century Victorian masonry buildings, the district transformed into a major entertainment hub in the 1970s and now hosts a dense concentration of bars, clubs, and restaurants. It is a central venue for world-renowned events like South by Southwest (SXSW) and remains a defining symbol of Austin's "Live Music Capital of the World" identity. Wikipedia), Visit Austin
attractions.address: East Sixth Street (between Congress Avenue and Interstate 35), Austin, TX 78701
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours (Street access); Individual establishments typically operate from 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM daily; Traffic closures often occur Thu-Sat starting at 9:00 PM Visit Austin, 6street.com
attractions.admission: Free (individual venues and special events may have cover charges or ticket prices) Viator
highlights: World-famous nightlife and bar-hopping scene between Congress Ave and I-35 Visit Austin
Historic Victorian-era architecture including the iconic Driskill Hotel Wikipedia)
Live music venues ranging from blues and jazz to rock and reggae Visit Austin
Central hub for the annual South by Southwest (SXSW) festival Wikipedia)
Pedestrian-only traffic on weekend nights (Thursday-Saturday) and major holidays Visit Austin
attractions.tips: Wear comfortable walking shoes as the street is often crowded and pedestrian-focused on weekends. Always carry a valid ID, as most venues are strictly 21+ in the evenings. Use rideshare services like Uber or Lyft to avoid difficult and expensive downtown parking. Stay aware of your surroundings and keep your belongings secure in high-traffic areas. Tripadvisor, Visit Austin
attractions.nearby: The Driskill Hotel, Texas State Capitol, Congress Avenue Bridge (Bats), Rainey Street, Lady Bird Lake Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Jack S. Blanton Museum of Art
attractions.description: The Jack S. Blanton Museum of Art at the University of Texas at Austin is one of the largest university art museums in the U.S. It features a world-class collection of more than 22,000 works, including significant holdings of modern, contemporary, and Latin American art, as well as European Old Master paintings. Wikipedia, Blanton Museum
attractions.address: 200 E. Martin Luther King Jr. Blvd., Austin, TX 78712
attractions.hours: Mon: Closed, Tue-Fri: 10am-5pm, Sat: 10am-8pm, Sun: 10am-5pm Blanton Museum
attractions.admission: Members: FREE, Adults: $15, Seniors (65+): $12, Youth (6–17): $8, Kids (5 & under): FREE, UT-Austin ID: FREE, Non-UT Austin College w/ID: $8, K-12 Teachers w/ID: FREE. Free admission every Tuesday. Blanton Museum
highlights: Austin by Ellsworth Kelly, a stone building with colored glass windows
Stacked Waters, a large-scale blue tile installation by Teresita Fernández
The Moody Patio, a redesigned outdoor plaza with petal-like shade structures
Extensive collection of Latin American and Contemporary Art
European Old Master paintings and prints
attractions.tips: Visit on Tuesdays for free admission. Advanced timed-entry tickets are recommended. The museum provides free lockers for bags at check-in. Paid parking is available at the Brazos Garage; validation is often available for a discount. Blanton Museum FAQ
attractions.nearby: Bullock Texas State History Museum, Texas State Capitol, LBJ Presidential Library, Harry Ransom Center, University of Texas at Austin campus. Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Mexic-Arte Museum Mexic-Arte Museum
attractions.description: The Mexic-Arte Museum is the Official Mexican and Mexican American Fine Art Museum of Texas, dedicated to enriching the community through the presentation and promotion of traditional and contemporary Mexican, Latino, and Latin American art and culture. Since its founding in 1984, the museum has served as a cultural hub in downtown Austin, offering a diverse range of exhibitions, educational programs, and community events like the annual Viva La Vida Festival. Mexic-Arte Museum, Austin Convention & Visitors Bureau
attractions.address: 419 Congress Ave, Austin, TX 78701 Mexic-Arte Museum
attractions.region: Downtown Austin / 5th Street Mexican American Heritage Corridor Mexic-Arte Museum
attractions.hours: Mon – Thu: 10 AM – 6 PM; Fri & Sat: 10 AM – 5 PM; Sun: 12 PM – 5 PM Mexic-Arte Museum
attractions.admission: Adults: $8; Seniors, Active Military, Veterans, Students, & Educators: $5; Children (12 & under): $1; Members: Free; Sundays: Free for everyone Mexic-Arte Museum
highlights: El Mero Muro project featuring rotating outdoor murals on the building's exterior Austin Convention & Visitors Bureau
Annual Viva La Vida Festival and Parade, Austin's largest and longest-running Day of the Dead celebration Mexic-Arte Museum
Permanent collection of over 5,000 contemporary Mexican, Latinx, and Latin American artworks Mexic-Arte Museum
Exhibitions focusing on contemporary social issues and cultural identity Mexic-Arte Museum
Changarrito Residency program supporting emerging artists Mexic-Arte Museum
attractions.tips: Visit on Sundays for free admission. Discounted parking is available at the Frost Bank Tower Garage (4 hours for $10) with a museum validation. Note that food, drinks, and backpacks are prohibited inside the galleries. Mexic-Arte Museum
attractions.nearby: Frost Bank Tower, Republic Square, The Contemporary Austin - Jones Center, Driskill Hotel, Congress Avenue Bridge (Bat Colony), 5th Street Mexican American Heritage Corridor Mexic-Arte Museum, Wikipedia

attractions.name: Paramount Theatre
attractions.description: Opened in 1915, the Paramount Theatre is a historic performing arts venue and movie palace located in the heart of downtown Austin. It hosts over 550 events annually, including live theater, comedy, and the iconic Summer Classic Film Series (Paramount Theatre).
attractions.address: 713 Congress Avenue, Austin, TX 78701
attractions.hours: Box Office: Monday–Friday, Noon–5:30pm (remains open for show service); Saturday–Sunday, open 2 hours prior to showtime (Paramount Theatre)
attractions.days_open: Daily (Box Office open Mon-Fri; open weekends for scheduled events) (Paramount Theatre)
attractions.admission: Tickets start at approximately $10, with prices varying significantly by event and seating tier (Vivid Seats). Membership tiers with additional benefits are also available (Paramount Theatre).
highlights: Built in 1915 and listed on the National Register of Historic Places (Wikipedia))
Produces the Moontower Comedy Festival, one of the nation's largest comedy events (Paramount Theatre)
Hosts the Paramount Summer Classic Film Series, a beloved Austin tradition for over 50 years (Paramount Theatre)
Primary venue for star-studded world premieres at SXSW and the Austin Film Festival (Paramount Theatre)
Features a capacity of 1,273 seats with a beautifully restored classical revival interior (Vivid Seats)
attractions.tips: Arrive early as doors typically open 90 minutes to 2 hours before events; utilize the nearby 600 Congress Avenue Parking Garage; show your show ticket at partner restaurants like Quattro Gatti or HeyDey Social Club for dining discounts (Paramount Theatre).
attractions.nearby: The State Theatre, Texas State Capitol, The Driskill Hotel, Hyatt Centric Congress Avenue (Paramount Theatre)

attractions.name: Sixth Street Entertainment District
attractions.description: The Sixth Street Entertainment District is the historic heart of Austin's world-famous live music and nightlife scene. Listed on the National Register of Historic Places, this vibrant nine-block area features a dense collection of bars, restaurants, and music venues housed in preserved 19th-century Victorian buildings. Often compared to New Orleans' Bourbon Street, it is the city's premier destination for entertainment, festivals, and cultural events.
attractions.address: E. 6th St. (Congress Ave. to I-35), Austin, TX 78701
attractions.hours: Monday: 24 Hours; Tuesday: 24 Hours; Wednesday: 24 Hours; Thursday: 24 Hours; Friday: 24 Hours; Saturday: 24 Hours; Sunday: 24 Hours (Venues typically open late morning/afternoon and close at 2:00 AM)
attractions.admission: Free (Individual venues may have cover charges)
highlights: Vibrant nightlife with dozens of bars and live music venues within walking distance.
Historic Victorian-style architecture preserved within the Sixth Street Historic District.
Home to the semi-annual Pecan Street Festival, a massive arts and crafts fair.
World-class entertainment at venues like Esther's Follies (comedy/magic) and the historic Ritz theater.
Diverse dining options ranging from iconic dive bars like Casino El Camino to upscale eateries like Parkside.
Iconic landmarks including the historic Driskill Hotel located at the corner of 6th and Brazos.
attractions.tips: The street is typically closed to vehicular traffic on Thursday, Friday, and Saturday nights (9 PM - 3 AM) to accommodate pedestrian crowds. For a more relaxed experience with better access to history and dining, visit during the daytime or early evening. Parking is most reliable in nearby garages as street parking fills quickly. Stay aware of your surroundings during peak late-night hours.
attractions.nearby: The Driskill Hotel, Texas State Capitol, Congress Avenue, Rainey Street Historic District, Lady Bird Lake, Waterloo Greenway.

attractions.name: Sixth Street Historic District (Old Pecan Street)
attractions.description: Old Pecan Street, now known as the Sixth Street Historic District, is the historic heart of Austin's vibrant entertainment scene and nightlife. This nine-block area is famous for its well-preserved Victorian-era commercial architecture and its concentration of bars, live music venues, and eclectic eateries. As a primary cultural hub, it hosts major events like the bi-annual Pecan Street Festival, celebrating the street's original name and Austin's artistic spirit.
attractions.address: East 6th Street (between Congress Avenue and Interstate 35), Austin, TX 78701
attractions.hours: District: Open 24/7; Most bars and music venues: 5:00 PM - 2:00 AM daily (Yelp, Parkside).
attractions.admission: Free (Public District); Viator notes guided tours start at $25.
attractions.phone: 512-573-6194 (Pecan Street Association), 512-478-0098 (Austin Visitor Center) (Idealist, Visit Austin)
highlights: Historic Victorian-era masonry commercial architecture, recognized on the National Register of Historic Places (Wikipedia)).
Vibrant live music scene with legendary venues like Antone’s and The Parish nearby (Mount Bonnell).
Host of the bi-annual Pecan Street Festival, one of the nation's longest-running arts and music festivals (Pecan Street Festival).
Renowned nightlife destination, affectionately nicknamed 'Dirty 6th' for its rowdy and energetic atmosphere (6street.com).
Pedestrian-only access on weekend evenings when the street is closed to vehicular traffic for safety and crowd control (Viator).
Proximity to diverse dining options ranging from traditional Tex-Mex to fine dining at establishments like Parkside (Yelp).
attractions.tips: The area, especially 'Dirty 6th', is best visited during the day for history buffs and after dark for those seeking nightlife, though it is not recommended for children in the evening. Be aware that the street is often closed to traffic on Thursday, Friday, and Saturday nights, making public transit or ridesharing a better option than searching for parking (Viator).
attractions.nearby: Congress Avenue, Red River Cultural District, Rainey Street Historic District, and the Austin Visitor Center (6street.com).

attractions.name: Texas State Capitol
attractions.description: The Texas State Capitol is the seat of government for the state of Texas, housing the offices and chambers of the Texas Legislature and the Governor. Completed in 1888, the Italian Renaissance Revival structure is famous for its sunset red granite exterior and its dome, which stands taller than the U.S. Capitol. Wikipedia, Official Site
attractions.address: 1100 Congress Ave, Austin, Texas 78701
attractions.hours: Monday–Friday: 7:00 a.m. to 8:00 p.m.; Saturday–Sunday: 9:00 a.m. to 8:00 p.m. Official Site
attractions.admission: Free (Guided and self-guided tours are also free) Official Site, Trip.com
highlights: Sunset red granite exterior and massive 302-foot tall dome Wikipedia
Central rotunda featuring portraits of all past Texas presidents and governors Wikipedia
Whispering gallery effect within the dome's rotunda Wikipedia
Four-story underground Capitol Extension completed in 1993 Official Site
22 acres of landscaped grounds containing over 20 monuments Official Site
Intricate terrazzo mosaics of the seals of the six nations that have governed Texas Wikipedia
attractions.tips: Free guided tours leave from the South Foyer every 15-30 minutes; allow at least 1-2 hours for your visit. Visitors can park for free for the first two hours at the Capitol Visitors Parking Garage located at 1201 San Jacinto Blvd. Official Site, Trip.com
attractions.nearby: Bullock Texas State History Museum, Blanton Museum of Art, Texas Governor's Mansion, Texas State Cemetery, University of Texas at Austin Official Site, Trip.com

attractions.name: The Paramount Theatre
attractions.description: Opened in 1915 as a vaudeville house, the Paramount Theatre is a historic landmark and performing arts venue located in the heart of downtown Austin. For over a century, it has hosted a diverse range of entertainment, including silent films, world-class concerts, and major film premieres. As one of the few century-old theatres still in active use in the U.S., it remains the artistic heartbeat of the city. Paramount Theatre, Wikipedia)

highlights: Historic 1915 Classical Revival style structure listed on the National Register of Historic Places. Wikipedia)
Annual Summer Classic Film Series, now running for over 50 years. Paramount Theatre
The iconic "Paramount Blade" marquee sign, a 2015 replica of the original 1930s sign. Paramount Theatre
Home to major events like the Moontower Comedy Festival and SXSW Film Festival. Paramount Theatre
Rare original 1915 hand-painted fire curtain and ceiling murals. Paramount Theatre
Avenue of Paramount Stars honoring Texas artists outside the theatre doors. Paramount Theatre
attractions.tips: The venue operates with cashless bars for concessions. Adhere to the bag policy (max 14"x12"x6") for faster entry. Arrive early to view the Avenue of Paramount Stars outside and the neoclassical Saint Cecilia painting on the auditorium ceiling. Discounted $6 parking is often available at the 600 Congress garage for classic film attendees. Paramount Theatre

attractions.name: The University of Texas at Austin Main Building (UT Tower) The University of Texas at Austin
attractions.category: Landmark, University Building The University of Texas at Austin
attractions.description: The UT Tower is the iconic 307-foot centerpiece of the University of Texas at Austin, serving as a symbol of academic excellence and university pride. Completed in 1937 in the Beaux-Arts style, it houses administrative offices and the historic Life Science Library. The building is famous for its "burnt orange" lighting traditions and its 56-bell carillon, the largest in the state. The University of Texas at Austin, Wikipedia)
attractions.address: 110 Inner Campus Drive Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Mon-Fri: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Sat-Sun: Closed UT Libraries
attractions.admission: Free for building access; Tower observation deck tours are currently suspended due to restoration University Unions
attractions.phone: (512) 471-3434 The University of Texas at Austin
highlights: Stands 307 feet tall with 27 floors and a 56-bell carillon, the largest in Texas. Wikipedia)
Features the historic Life Science Library with grand reading rooms. UT Libraries
Famous for its "burnt orange" lighting used to celebrate university achievements. UT Tower Lightings
Designed by renowned architect Paul Philippe Cret in the Beaux-Arts style. Wikipedia)
Home to "Tower Girl," a resident peregrine falcon monitored via webcam. UT Biodiversity
attractions.tips: Official Tower observation deck tours are currently suspended for a multi-year restoration project scheduled through early 2027. Visitors can still enter the Main Building during business hours to see the Life Science Library. Check the official Tower lighting schedule online to see if the building will be illuminated during your visit. UT Tower FAQ, Construction Updates

attractions.name: Warehouse District
attractions.description: Austin's Warehouse District is a vibrant downtown neighborhood characterized by renovated industrial warehouses that now house a sophisticated array of restaurants, bars, and live music venues. Often referred to as the more mature counterpart to Sixth Street, it serves as the primary hub for the city's LGBTQ+ nightlife and offers a gritty yet hip urban atmosphere. Visit Austin, Expedia
attractions.address: W. 4th St (between Congress Ave and Guadalupe St), Austin, TX 78701
attractions.hours: Varies by business; nightlife venues typically active 4:00 PM – 2:00 AM, with cafes and retail opening earlier. Halcyon Austin
attractions.admission: Free (individual venues like clubs or bars may have cover charges) Visit Austin
highlights: Hub for iconic LGBTQ+ nightlife, including venues like Oilcan Harry’s and Rain on 4th. Visit Austin
Historic architecture featuring 19th-century brick warehouses repurposed into modern lofts and businesses. Austin Chronicle
Sophisticated dining destinations such as the Michelin-recommended Comedor and Hestia. Visit Austin
Premier jazz and live music at locations like Parker Jazz Club and Speakeasy. Visit Austin
Home to Republic Square, which hosts the weekly SFC Farmers’ Market and outdoor community events. Downtown Austin Alliance
attractions.tips: Known as "Sixth Street for grown-ups," this area is ideal for a more upscale or relaxed night out. Visit on Saturday mornings for the farmers' market at Republic Square. Parking is limited, so ride-sharing or using the nearby MetroRail is recommended. Visit Austin, Apartments.com

attractions.name: Ann W. Richards Congress Avenue Bridge Bat Colony
attractions.description: The Ann W. Richards Congress Avenue Bridge is home to the world's largest urban bat colony, hosting up to 1.5 million Mexican free-tailed bats. Every evening from spring to late fall, the bats emerge from the bridge's crevices at sunset to hunt insects, creating a spectacular natural display that attracts thousands of visitors to Lady Bird Lake. Wikipedia, Bat Conservation International
attractions.address: 305 S Congress Ave
attractions.hours: Monday-Sunday: Open 24 hours (Bat emergence typically occurs between 7:30 PM and 9:45 PM). AustinTexas.org
attractions.days_open: Daily (March through November for bat viewing; site open year-round) AustinTexas.org
attractions.admission: Free for viewing from the bridge or the Statesman Bat Observation Center; boat tours range from $13.00 to $14.00 for adults. Austin City Guide, Capital Cruises
highlights: Home to the largest urban bat colony in the world. Wikipedia
Features a maternity colony of up to 1.5 million Mexican free-tailed bats. Texas Parks and Wildlife
Nightly emergence at sunset from March through November. AustinTexas.org
Viewing available from the bridge, the Statesman Bat Observation Center, or Lady Bird Lake. Austin City Guide
A central part of Austin's 'Keep Austin Weird' cultural identity. Wikipedia
attractions.tips: Arrive 30 to 60 minutes before sunset to secure a good viewing spot; bring a blanket or lawn chairs for the Statesman Bat Observation Center; check recent emergence times online as the bats keep their own schedule. Bat Conservation International, AustinTexas.org
attractions.nearby: Lady Bird Lake, Ann and Roy Butler Hike-and-Bike Trail, South Congress Avenue (SoCo) District, Auditorium Shores. Tripadvisor, Austin City Guide

attractions.name: Jo's Coffee South Congress
attractions.description: Jo’s Coffee opened on South Congress Avenue in 1999 and has since become a quintessential Austin landmark known for its coffee, tacos, and prime people-watching. As a hub of community activity, it regularly hosts fundraisers, concerts, and local gatherings in its convivial, down-to-earth locale. The shop is most famous for the "I Love You So Much" mural on its exterior wall, which attracts visitors from around the world (Jo's Coffee, I Love South Congress).

attractions.hours: 7:00 AM – 7:00 PM Daily (Jo's Coffee)
attractions.days_open: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday
attractions.admission: Free entry; prices vary for food and beverages (Jo's Coffee).
highlights: Iconic "I Love You So Much" mural on the exterior wall (I Love South Congress).
Signature "Belgian Bomber" iced coffee beverage (Tripadvisor).
Dog-friendly outdoor patio and community hub (Jo's Coffee).
Prime people-watching location on the bustling South Congress Avenue (Jo's Coffee).
Selection of local favorites including breakfast tacos and sandwiches (Jo's Coffee).
Host of popular community events like the annual Pet Parade and Jo's Chili Cook-Off (Jo's Coffee).
attractions.tips: Arrive early in the morning or during the week to avoid the longest lines for the mural. Since the seating is entirely outdoors, check the weather before visiting. Don't miss the Belgian Bomber for a unique caffeine fix, and feel free to bring your dog along (I Love South Congress, Jo's Coffee).

attractions.name: Franklin Barbecue
attractions.description: Franklin Barbecue is a world-renowned barbecue destination in Austin, Texas (Franklin Barbecue), widely considered one of the best in the country (Female Foodie). Founded by Aaron Franklin in 2009 (Wikipedia), the restaurant is famous for its slow-smoked brisket and legendary long lines that form hours before opening (Texas Monthly). It has received numerous accolades, including a James Beard Award, and has hosted famous guests such as President Barack Obama (Wikipedia).

attractions.name: Elisabet Ney Museum
attractions.description: The Elisabet Ney Museum is the historic home and studio of Elisabet Ney, a trailblazing German-American sculptor who moved to Austin in 1882. Housed in her limestone sanctuary named Formosa, the museum showcases her life, tools, and the world's largest collection of her neoclassical portrait sculptures. (City of Austin, National Park Service)
attractions.address: 304 East 44th Street, Austin, TX 78751
attractions.region: Hyde Park National Register Historic Neighborhood (City of Austin)
attractions.hours: Wednesday - Sunday: 12:00 PM - 5:00 PM (Note: Closed for renovations starting December 30, 2024; anticipated reopening Fall 2026) (City of Austin, Austin Convention & Visitors Bureau)
attractions.days_open: Wednesday - Sunday (Standard operating days; currently closed for renovations until late 2026) (City of Austin, Austin Convention & Visitors Bureau)
attractions.admission: Free (City of Austin, Austin Convention & Visitors Bureau)
highlights: Home to the world's largest collection of sculptor Elisabet Ney's work, including portrait busts of Sam Houston and Stephen F. Austin. (City of Austin)
The building, named Formosa, was the earliest art studio built in Texas and is listed on the National Register of Historic Places. (Wikipedia)
Features a 2.5-acre site with a recreated native prairie biome reflecting the landscape as it appeared in the late 19th century. (City of Austin)
Includes a unique stone tower built as a workspace for Ney's husband, Dr. Edmund Montgomery. (National Park Service)
The studio historically served as a cultural salon for influential Texans to discuss art, politics, and philosophy. (Texas Historical Commission)
attractions.tips: Read about Elisabet Ney’s fascinating life before visiting to appreciate her radical story. Street parking is available, and visitors should allow about 45-90 minutes for a full tour. Be sure to check for current renovation status before planning a visit. (Tripadvisor)
attractions.nearby: Shipe Park (featuring a pool and playground), LBJ Presidential Library, Texas State Capitol, Bullock Texas State History Museum, and Thinkery. (Tripadvisor, Austin Active Kids)

attractions.name: Austin Nature and Science Center
attractions.description: The Austin Nature and Science Center is a hands-on educational sanctuary dedicated to the exploration and stewardship of the natural world. Since 1960, it has provided visitors of all ages with immersive exhibits, wildlife encounters, and nature-based programs within the Zilker Nature Preserve (Official Website).
attractions.address: 2389 Stratford Drive, Austin, TX 78746
attractions.hours: Monday–Saturday: 9:00 a.m. – 5:00 p.m.; Sunday: 12:00 p.m. – 5:00 p.m. (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Free (Official Website)
highlights: Dino Pit: A large outdoor sand pit where visitors can dig for replica dinosaur fossils (Official Website)
Wildlife Exhibits: Rescued native Texas animals including bobcats, porcupines, and various birds of prey (Official Website)
Naturalist Workshop & Trade Counter: A unique space where visitors can trade natural items they've found for points to buy fossils, rocks, and minerals (Official Website)
Forest Trail: A self-guided trail featuring 45 native Texas trees designed to educate home gardeners (Official Website)
Human Sundial: An interactive exhibit where visitors use their own shadows to tell time (Official Website)
Small Wonders Building: Features terrariums and aquariums highlighting the smallest native Central Texas creatures (Official Website)
attractions.tips: Parking is free and located under the Mopac (Loop 1) bridge across from the entrance. No pets are allowed on site. If visiting with children, consider bringing a change of clothes as they may get wet or dirty in the Dino Pit or creek areas. The Trade Counter has specific hours and does not accept items found on-site (Official Website).

attractions.description: The Frank Erwin Center was a prominent multi-purpose arena located on the University of Texas at Austin campus that served as the home for Longhorns basketball and premier entertainment events from 1977 to 2022 (Wikipedia). Known affectionately as "The Drum" for its distinctive round architecture, it hosted legendary concerts, sporting events, and graduation ceremonies before being replaced by the Moody Center and demolished in 2024 (Texas Longhorns, CBS Austin).

attractions.region: Downtown / University of Texas Campus (Tripadvisor, Wikipedia)

attractions.name: Lady Bird Lake (Wikipedia)
attractions.description: Lady Bird Lake is a river-like reservoir on the Colorado River in Austin, Texas, originally created in 1960 as a cooling pond for a city power plant. Named in honor of former First Lady Lady Bird Johnson, it now serves as a major recreational hub and nature escape in the heart of the city. The lake is a popular destination for non-motorized water sports and is surrounded by a scenic 10-mile hike-and-bike trail. (Wikipedia, Viator)

attractions.tips: Swimming is illegal in Lady Bird Lake due to safety concerns and underwater debris. Only non-motorized watercraft (or those with electric motors under 5 hp) are permitted on the water. The hike-and-bike trail is extremely popular, so it is best to visit early or on weekdays to avoid the largest crowds. (Viator, AustinTexas.gov)

The museum's extensive collection explores the life and legacy of the 36th President, featuring permanent exhibits that connect his legislative achievements to contemporary American life (LBJ Library, Wikipedia).

attractions.admission: Admission varies by show; many happy hour performances have no cover, while evening shows typically range from $8 to $15. Larger ticketed events can cost $25–$35 or more (The Continental Club, The Continental Club Tickets). Note that door cover charges are cash only (Tripadvisor).
highlights: Recognized as a historic landmark by the City of Austin (The Continental Club)
A premier destination for roots, rockabilly, country, swing, rock, and blues music (The Continental Club)
Features a iconic flashing neon sign that is a landmark on South Congress Avenue (The Continental Club)
Home to restored 1950s era murals that line the interior walls (The Continental Club)
Has hosted music legends such as Stevie Ray Vaughan, Robert Plant, and Junior Brown (The Continental Club)
Named one of the top 10 music venues in America by Rolling Stone (YouTube - The Texas Bucket List)
attractions.tips: Bring cash for the door as cover charges are cash only. Seating is very limited in the small venue, so arrive early if you want a table. Check the daily schedule for happy hour shows which often have no cover charge. Most evening performances are strictly 21+ only (Tripadvisor, The Continental Club Tickets).
attractions.nearby: Congress Avenue Bridge (Bat watching), Jo's Coffee, Allens Boots, Hopdoddy Burger Bar, and C-Boy's Heart & Soul (Austin Insider Blog)

attractions.name: The Salt Lick BBQ
attractions.description: The Salt Lick BBQ is a legendary Texas Hill Country destination that has been serving world-renowned barbecue since 1967. Guests are greeted by a famous open fire pit where meats are slow-cooked over oak coals and finished with a unique, caramelizing sauce. The rustic setting features century-old oak trees, rolling hills, and a welcoming atmosphere that captures the essence of Texas history. Salt Lick BBQ Official Site
attractions.address: 18300 FM 1826, Driftwood, TX 78619
attractions.hours: Sunday – Thursday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM; Friday – Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM Salt Lick BBQ Hours
attractions.admission: Free (Meal prices range from $10.95 to $33.95+; Family Style is $33.95 per person) Salt Lick BBQ Menu
highlights: Famous open-fire barbecue pit used since 1967
All-you-can-eat Family Style dining option
Scenic Texas Hill Country setting with live music on the patio
BYOB policy (at the Driftwood location) with Salt Lick Cellars nearby
Signature barbecue sauce and dry rubs used for six generations
Designated a Texas Treasure Business in 2022
attractions.tips: The Driftwood location is cash-only (ATM available on-site), but they do accept credit cards for mail orders and at other locations. It is BYOB, so feel free to bring your own coolers or purchase beverages at the adjacent Salt Lick Cellars. For large groups of 10 or more, reservations are accepted Monday through Thursday via Resy. Salt Lick BBQ FAQ
attractions.nearby: Salt Lick Cellars, Fall Creek Vineyards, Camp Ben McCulloch, Duchman Family Winery, Desert Door Distillery, Vista Brewing Tripadvisor

attractions.name: The University of Texas at Austin
attractions.description: Founded in 1883, The University of Texas at Austin is the flagship institution of the University of Texas System and a world-renowned public research university. The campus, known as the "Forty Acres," features iconic architecture, seven museums, and seventeen libraries, serving as a major cultural and academic hub in the heart of Texas. Wikipedia, UT Austin website
attractions.address: 110 Inner Campus Drive, Austin, TX 78712
attractions.hours: Campus grounds: Open 24/7. Admissions Welcome Center: Mon-Fri 8:30 a.m. – 4:00 p.m. General building hours: Mon-Fri 8:00 a.m. – 5:00 p.m. Admissions website, UT Austin website
attractions.admission: Free (general campus grounds and self-guided tours); individual museums and attractions like the Blanton Museum of Art or LBJ Library may have separate fees. Harry Ransom Center, AustinTexas.org
highlights: UT Tower: The university's most recognizable landmark and symbol.
LBJ Presidential Library: A museum covering President Lyndon B. Johnson's political career and American history.
Blanton Museum of Art: One of the largest university art museums in the country.
Harry Ransom Center: Home to a Gutenberg Bible and other rare literary artifacts.
Darrell K Royal-Texas Memorial Stadium: Home of the Texas Longhorns football team.
The Drag: A vibrant section of Guadalupe Street filled with shops, restaurants, and murals.
attractions.tips: Public access to campus grounds is free, but visitor parking is restricted to designated garages; avoid street parking without a permit. Don't miss the 'The Color Inside' Skyspace at sunset for a unique light experience. The Harry Ransom Center offers free admission to view rare treasures like the Gutenberg Bible. KD College Prep, Parking & Transportation
attractions.nearby: Texas State Capitol, Bullock Texas State History Museum, Pease District Park, Waterloo Park, Downtown Austin. Visit Austin

attractions.name: Travis Heights (Travis Heights-Fairview Park Historic District)
attractions.description: Travis Heights is a historic neighborhood in south Austin, Texas, known for its unique blend of early 20th-century mansions, quaint bungalows, and modern flair. Founded in 1912, it is characterized by winding tree-lined streets, abundant green spaces like the Blunn Creek Nature Preserve, and its close proximity to the vibrant South Congress (SoCo) district. It remains one of Austin's most desirable and picturesque residential areas, offering a mix of natural beauty and urban convenience.
attractions.address: Bounded by Lady Bird Lake (North), I-35 (East), Congress Ave (West), and Oltorf St (South)
attractions.hours: Parks (Big Stacy, Little Stacy, Blunn Creek): Mon-Sun 5:00 AM - 10:00 PM; Big Stacy Pool: Weekdays 6:00 AM - 8:00 PM, Weekends 12:00 PM - 7:00 PM (Summer hours)
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: Historic Travis Heights-Fairview Park District architecture and charm.
Big Stacy and Little Stacy Parks, including a spring-fed swimming pool.
Blunn Creek Nature Preserve with 40 acres of scenic trails and historic plaques.
Proximity to the South Congress (SoCo) district's eclectic shops and cafes.
Walking distance to Lady Bird Lake and the Ann and Roy Butler Hike-and-Bike Trail.
The annual Travis Heights Art Trail showcasing local creative talent.
attractions.tips: Park on residential side streets for easy access to Blunn Creek trails or Little Stacy Park. Visit Big Stacy Pool for a free, geothermally heated swim year-round. Don't miss the famous Austin murals like 'I Love You So Much' located just a short walk away on South Congress.
attractions.nearby: South Congress Avenue (SoCo), Lady Bird Lake, Downtown Austin, Zilker Park, Auditorium Shores.

attractions.name: UMLAUF Sculpture Garden + Museum (Official Website)
attractions.description: The UMLAUF Sculpture Garden + Museum is a serene six-acre outdoor museum in Austin, Texas, showcasing the bronze sculptures of Charles Umlauf alongside rotating contemporary exhibitions. It offers a unique blend of nature and art, allowing visitors to touch the sculptures and enjoy peaceful garden paths. The garden features winding pathways, tranquil ponds, and a waterfall, providing a peaceful escape in the heart of the city (Official Website, Austin Insider Blog).
attractions.address: 605 Azie Morton Road, Austin, Texas 78704 (Official Website)
attractions.hours: Tuesday - Friday: 10 AM — 4 PM, Saturday - Sunday: 11 AM — 4 PM, Monday: Closed (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Adult: $8, Senior (60+): $6, Active Military & Veteran: $4, K-12 Teacher: $4, College Student: $4, Youth (K-12): $2, Child (0-4 yrs): Free, Museums for All (with EBT card & photo ID): Free for family, Members: Free (Official Website)
highlights: Over 40 bronze and stone sculptures by Charles Umlauf on permanent display (Official Website)
Touchable bronze sculptures, a unique feature designed for all visitors including those with visual impairments (Official Website)
Six acres of lush landscaping featuring a waterfall, streams, and tranquil ponds (Official Website)
Indoor gallery featuring rotating contemporary sculpture exhibitions (Official Website)
Family-friendly 'Sculpture Searches' and interactive educational programs (Official Website)
UMLAUF After Dark series with live music and evening access (Official Website)
attractions.tips: Photography is encouraged, but smoking, food, and drinks are not permitted; dogs are only allowed if they are service animals (Official Website). Parking is limited in the main lot, so consider overflow parking at Wright Field or nearby Zilker lots (Official Website). Visitors typically spend between 30 and 90 minutes exploring the grounds (Official Website).
attractions.nearby: Barton Springs Pool, Zilker Metropolitan Park, Lady Bird Lake (Official Website, Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: West Sixth Street Entertainment District
attractions.description: West Sixth Street is a sophisticated and vibrant stretch of downtown Austin known for its relaxed mix of upscale nightlife, fine dining, and boutique shopping. Unlike the rowdier "Dirty Sixth" to the east, this district offers a more polished atmosphere with chic cocktail lounges, artisanal bakeries, and historic architecture. It serves as a central hub for locals and visitors looking to experience Austin's modern culture alongside its historic charm.
attractions.address: W 6th St (from Congress Ave to MoPac), Austin, TX 78701
attractions.hours: District open 24/7; Bars typically 4:00 PM – 2:00 AM daily; Restaurant and shop hours vary by establishment
attractions.admission: Free to explore; individual venue prices vary
highlights: Historic West Sixth Street Bridge over Shoal Creek, a masonry arch bridge built in 1887
Flagship Whole Foods Market, a major culinary and shopping destination in the district
Iconic local eateries such as Clark's Oyster Bar and the 24-hour comfort food at 24 Diner
Sophisticated nightlife at long-standing favorites like Star Bar and Little Woodrow’s
Boutique shopping at designer clothing stores like ByGeorge and the famous BookPeople bookstore
Contemporary art galleries including West Chelsea Contemporary and Lotus Gallery
attractions.tips: Street parking is very limited; visitors are encouraged to use rideshares, bikes, or electric scooters. The district is more laid-back on weekdays but becomes high-energy on weekend nights.
attractions.nearby: Texas State Capitol, Lady Bird Lake, Zilker Park, Seaholm District, Clarksville Neighborhood

attractions.name: Zilker Metropolitan Park (City of Austin)
attractions.description: Zilker Metropolitan Park is Austin's oldest and most beloved metropolitan park, spanning over 350 acres of publicly owned land at the juncture of Barton Creek and Lady Bird Lake. Often referred to as the "crown jewel" of the Austin parks system, it serves as a central hub for recreational activities, cultural events, and natural preservation (City of Austin, Wikipedia)
attractions.address: 2100 Barton Springs Rd, Austin, TX 78704 (City of Austin, NowPlayingAustin)
attractions.region: South Austin / Barton Hills (Wikipedia, Apple Maps)
attractions.hours: 5:00 AM – 10:00 PM Daily (City of Austin, Apple Maps)
attractions.admission: General admission to the park is free. Fees apply for specific attractions: Barton Springs Pool (Residents: $2-$5, Non-Residents: $4-$9) and Zilker Botanical Garden (Residents: $3-$6, Non-Residents: $4-$8). Weekend parking is $3 per hour from Spring Break through Labor Day (City of Austin, Zilker Botanical Garden)
highlights: Barton Springs Pool, a 3-acre natural spring-fed pool that maintains a year-round temperature of 68-70 degrees (Visit Austin)
Zilker Botanical Garden, featuring 26 landscaped areas including the Taniguchi Japanese Garden and Hartman Prehistoric Garden (Spyglass Realty)
The Zilker Eagle miniature train, providing scenic tours around the park's major attractions (Wikipedia)
The Great Lawn, which hosts major events like the Austin City Limits Music Festival and the ABC Kite Festival (City of Austin)
Austin Nature & Science Center, offering hands-on exhibits and a dinosaur "dino pit" for children (Visit Austin)
Umlauf Sculpture Garden & Museum, showcasing the works of artist Charles Umlauf in a serene outdoor setting (Wikipedia)
attractions.tips: Visitors are encouraged to use public transit or bikes as parking is limited during peak times. Download the Park ATX app to pay for parking easily, and remember that Barton Springs Pool is closed for cleaning on Thursdays (City of Austin, Wikipedia)
attractions.nearby: Lady Bird Lake, Ann and Roy Butler Hike-and-Bike Trail, South Lamar shopping and dining district, and the Barton Hills neighborhood (Spyglass Realty, Visit Austin)
